titleStmt.title: SII 11.11: original edition by C. R. Krishnamacharlu, R. S. Panchamukhi, N. Lakshminaraya Rao and H. K. Narasimhaswami
No. 11. (B.K. No. 60 of 1927-28). MĒVUṆḌI, MUNDARGI PETHA, DHARWAR DISTRICT. ON A SLAB SET UP IN FRONT OF THE BASAVAṆṆA TEMPLE.

summary.p: The inscription refers itself to the reign of Amōghavarsha I and is dated in Śaka [7]86 (= A.D. 865). It mentions Kuppeyarasa of the Yādava-vaṁśa. From the Soraṭūr inscription (Ep. Ind., Vol. XIII, p. 77) we know that he was ruling the Purigeṟe-nāḍu as a feudatory of the king. It is evident from the present record that ‘Ādava’ the family name of this chief occurring in the Soraṭūr epigraph is only a corruption of ‘Yādava’.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© svastyamōghavarṣa śrīpṛthuvīvalla
bha mahārājādhirājaṁ paramēśvara bhaṭā[*ra]
kar pṛthuvīrājyaṁgeye svasti pracaṇḍama
ṇḍalāgrakhaṇḍitārātimaṇḍalapratāpaSome lines seem to have been lost after this.

sudagganuḻedōṁ saḷesu(ṁ)nduḻivōṁ tandeya tāyvirā sa
ntāna ma(ṁ)nuḷidu bisuṭōṁ maṟuvārttegoḷḷan
bendiyuṁ kaḷacumāṟaḍiyu māgaḷidane kādi
tannanāḷdāta saye bāḻanārttanna bēḍido
ḍe kuḍuguṁ nikkuva tōṁṭamamane
nintaṟi suṣṭhura ḻdō n
saṁpanna yādava vaṁśō rvva
dhavaḷa ṣaḍguṇāḷaṁ di
śrīmatkuppeyarasara
maga naṇḍuga raṭṭiyaṇna
saṁ beḷāvaḍica mēḻemaḍuvi
ḻdu Arasana meyge karameṁbudaṁ kēḷdu
llamundanindu kūḻā toṟadu mūvattu gāvuda
devasadoḷeydi Arasanaṁ jāmisi
nurivuttiḻdudaṁ kaṇḍu kīḻguṇṭeyāgalkuḍadu da
maṁ kuḍu venendu piḍikhaṇḍaṁ goṇḍu desevaligeThe letter ḍu is engraved below the line.
ṟapoḻdu karuḷa bōniḍigalloḷikki Eḻdiya
mare palarmmāḍi Ādityaṁgetti Ittakke me
ḷgellatōṟi bērandaṟiyisi toṭṭirddutaṁbu
sūṟuguḻdu Ākimāriveccaḍe rvi sakava
ṟa Eṇbhattāḻaneya varṣaṁ pōdabaḻikke nindudīkallu
purigeṟeyiṁ banda poleyaṇnana śilākarmmaLines 26 and 27 are engraved at the top of this face.
Arakuḷiyana kalluvokallu ©
Digital edition of SII 11.11 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
